CREATE TABLE test_files (id INT PRIMARY KEY, file_path VARCHAR(255), file_content BLOB);
INSERT INTO test_files (id, file_path, file_content) VALUES (1, '/index.html', 'Sample HTML content');
INSERT INTO test_files (id, file_path, file_content) VALUES (2, '/about.html', 'About us content');
INSERT INTO test_files (id, file_path, file_content) VALUES (3, '/contact.html', 'Contact us content');
SELECT file_path FROM test_files WHERE id = 1;
SELECT file_path FROM test_files WHERE file_path LIKE '/%';
SELECT DISTINCT file_path FROM test_files WHERE file_path IS NOT NULL;
SELECT * FROM test_files WHERE file_path IN (SELECT file_path FROM test_files WHERE id < 3);
SELECT * FROM test_files WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_files WHERE id = 2);
SELECT id, file_path FROM test_files WHERE id = (SELECT MAX(id) FROM test_files);
UPDATE test_files SET file_content = 'Updated content for index.html' WHERE file_path = '/index.html';
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT * FROM test_files WHERE file_path = '/about.html') THEN 'File exists' ELSE 'File not found' END AS file_check;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_files WHERE file_path LIKE '/%.html';
SELECT file_path, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_files) AS total_files FROM test_files WHERE id = 1;
SELECT * FROM test_files WHERE file_path IN (SELECT file_path FROM test_files WHERE id > 0) ORDER BY id DESC;
